paracetamol.name: Paracetamol
paracetamol.purpose: Used to reduce fever and relieve mild to moderate pain such as headache, toothache, and body ache
paracetamol.warning: Do not exceed 4g (8 tablets) per day. Avoid alcohol. May cause liver damage in overdose. Consult doctor if fever persists beyond 3 days.

amoxicillin.name: Amoxicillin
amoxicillin.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections including ear, nose, throat, urinary, and skin infections
amoxicillin.warning: Complete the full course even if you feel better. May cause allergic reactions (rash, swelling). Inform doctor if allergic to penicillin.

azithromycin.name: Azithromycin
azithromycin.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat respiratory infections, ear infections, and skin infections
azithromycin.warning: Complete the full course. May cause nausea or diarrhea. Avoid antacids 2 hours before or after taking this medicine.

cetirizine.name: Cetirizine
cetirizine.purpose: Antihistamine used to relieve allergy symptoms like runny nose, sneezing, itching, and watery eyes
cetirizine.warning: May cause drowsiness. Avoid driving or operating heavy machinery. Do not take with alcohol.

ibuprofen.name: Ibuprofen
ibuprofen.purpose: Used to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever. Commonly used for headache, dental pain, and muscle pain
ibuprofen.warning: Take with food to avoid stomach irritation. Not recommended for patients with stomach ulcers or kidney problems. Avoid long-term use.

metformin.name: Metformin
metformin.purpose: Used to control blood sugar levels in Type 2 diabetes
metformin.warning: Take with meals. May cause stomach upset initially. Do not skip doses. Regular blood sugar monitoring is recommended.

pantoprazole.name: Pantoprazole
pantoprazole.purpose: Used to reduce stomach acid. Treats acidity, heartburn, and gastric ulcers
pantoprazole.warning: Take on an empty stomach, 30 minutes before meals. Long-term use may affect calcium absorption. Consult doctor for use beyond 8 weeks.

omeprazole.name: Omeprazole
omeprazole.purpose: Used to treat acid reflux, heartburn, and stomach ulcers by reducing acid production
omeprazole.warning: Take before meals. Not recommended for long-term use without doctor's advice. May interact with blood thinners.

ranitidine.name: Ranitidine
ranitidine.purpose: Used to reduce stomach acid and treat acidity, heartburn, and ulcers
ranitidine.warning: Take 30 minutes before meals. Avoid excessive use. Consult doctor if symptoms persist.

aspirin.name: Aspirin
aspirin.purpose: Used as a pain reliever, anti-inflammatory, and blood thinner to prevent heart attacks
aspirin.warning: Not recommended for children under 16. Take with food. May increase bleeding risk. Inform doctor before any surgery.

metronidazole.name: Metronidazole
metronidazole.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat infections caused by bacteria and parasites, including dental and stomach infections
metronidazole.warning: Strictly avoid alcohol during and 48 hours after treatment. Complete the full course. May cause metallic taste.

ofloxacin.name: Ofloxacin
ofloxacin.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections of the urinary tract, respiratory tract, and skin
ofloxacin.warning: Avoid excessive sun exposure. Stay well hydrated. May cause dizziness. Complete the full course.

ciprofloxacin.name: Ciprofloxacin
ciprofloxacin.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat urinary tract infections, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal infections
ciprofloxacin.warning: Avoid dairy products and antacids near dosing time. Stay hydrated. May cause tendon problems in rare cases.

diclofenac.name: Diclofenac
diclofenac.purpose: Used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in conditions like arthritis, sprains, and injuries
diclofenac.warning: Take with food. Avoid in patients with heart or kidney problems. Not for long-term use without doctor's advice.

amlodipine.name: Amlodipine
amlodipine.purpose: Used to treat high blood pressure and chest pain (angina)
amlodipine.warning: Do not stop suddenly. May cause swelling in ankles. Take at the same time daily. Avoid grapefruit juice.

atenolol.name: Atenolol
atenolol.purpose: Used to treat high blood pressure and prevent heart attacks
atenolol.warning: Do not stop suddenly — may worsen heart condition. May cause dizziness. Take regularly at the same time.

losartan.name: Losartan
losartan.purpose: Used to treat high blood pressure and protect kidneys in diabetic patients
losartan.warning: May cause dizziness when standing up. Avoid potassium supplements. Not safe during pregnancy.

atorvastatin.name: Atorvastatin
atorvastatin.purpose: Used to lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease
atorvastatin.warning: Take at night for best results. Avoid excessive alcohol. Report any unexplained muscle pain to doctor.

levothyroxine.name: Levothyroxine
levothyroxine.purpose: Used to treat hypothyroidism (underactive thyroid gland)
levothyroxine.warning: Take on an empty stomach, 30-60 minutes before breakfast. Do not stop without doctor's advice. Regular thyroid tests needed.

montelukast.name: Montelukast
montelukast.purpose: Used to prevent asthma attacks and relieve seasonal allergies
montelukast.warning: Take in the evening. Not for acute asthma attacks. Report mood changes to doctor immediately.

salbutamol.name: Salbutamol
salbutamol.purpose: Inhaler medicine used to relieve breathing difficulty in asthma and COPD
salbutamol.warning: Use only when needed. Shake inhaler before use. Rinse mouth after use. Seek emergency help if breathing worsens.

domperidone.name: Domperidone
domperidone.purpose: Used to treat nausea, vomiting, and bloating
domperidone.warning: Take 15-30 minutes before meals. Not recommended for long-term use. May cause dry mouth.

ondansetron.name: Ondansetron
ondansetron.purpose: Used to prevent nausea and vomiting caused by surgery, chemotherapy, or other medications
ondansetron.warning: May cause headache or constipation. Take as directed by doctor. Inform doctor about heart conditions.

loperamide.name: Loperamide
loperamide.warning: Do not use for more than 2 days without doctor's advice. Stay well hydrated. Not for use in children under 6.

vitamin-c.name: Vitamin C
vitamin-c.purpose: Supplement to boost immunity and support healing. Helps in recovery from cold and flu
vitamin-c.warning: High doses may cause stomach upset. Take with food. Generally safe for most people.

vitamin-b-complex.name: Vitamin B Complex
vitamin-b-complex.purpose: Supplement containing B vitamins to support energy, nerve function, and overall health
vitamin-b-complex.warning: May cause bright yellow urine (harmless). Take with food for better absorption.

vitamin-d.name: Vitamin D
vitamin-d.purpose: Supplement to strengthen bones and support calcium absorption
vitamin-d.warning: Take with food containing fat for better absorption. Excessive intake may cause calcium buildup.

calcium.name: Calcium
calcium.purpose: Supplement to strengthen bones and teeth, prevent osteoporosis
calcium.warning: Take with Vitamin D for better absorption. May cause constipation. Space out from other medications.

iron.name: Iron
iron.purpose: Supplement to treat or prevent iron deficiency anemia
iron.warning: Take on an empty stomach with Vitamin C for better absorption. May cause dark stools (harmless). Avoid tea/coffee near dosing time.

folic-acid.name: Folic Acid
folic-acid.purpose: Vitamin supplement essential during pregnancy for baby's brain development. Also treats folate deficiency
folic-acid.warning: Safe for most people. Take as directed. Important to start before and during early pregnancy.

ibuprofen-paracetamol.name: Ibuprofen + Paracetamol (Combiflam)
ibuprofen-paracetamol.purpose: Combination pain reliever used for headache, toothache, body pain, and fever
ibuprofen-paracetamol.warning: Take with food. Do not exceed recommended dose. Avoid in patients with stomach ulcers or liver/kidney problems.

amoxicillin-clavulanate.name: Amoxicillin + Clavulanate (Augmentin)
amoxicillin-clavulanate.purpose: Strong antibiotic used for severe bacterial infections including sinusitis, pneumonia, and urinary infections
amoxicillin-clavulanate.warning: Complete the full course. Take with food. May cause diarrhea. Inform doctor if allergic to penicillin.

cefixime.name: Cefixime
cefixime.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections of the ear, nose, throat, and urinary tract
cefixime.warning: Complete the full course. May cause stomach upset. Inform doctor about any drug allergies.

doxycycline.name: Doxycycline
doxycycline.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat acne, respiratory infections, and urinary infections
doxycycline.warning: Avoid sun exposure. Take with plenty of water. Do not lie down for 30 minutes after taking. Avoid dairy near dosing time.

clindamycin.name: Clindamycin
clindamycin.purpose: Antibiotic used to treat serious bacterial infections including skin, bone, and dental infections
clindamycin.warning: Complete the full course. Report severe diarrhea immediately. Take with a full glass of water.

glimepiride.name: Glimepiride
glimepiride.purpose: Used to control blood sugar levels in Type 2 diabetes by stimulating insulin release
glimepiride.warning: Take with breakfast. May cause low blood sugar — carry sugar/candy. Do not skip meals.

metoprolol.name: Metoprolol
metoprolol.purpose: Used to treat high blood pressure, chest pain, and heart failure
metoprolol.warning: Do not stop suddenly. Take with food. May cause fatigue or dizziness. Monitor heart rate regularly.

clopidogrel.name: Clopidogrel
clopidogrel.purpose: Blood thinner used to prevent heart attacks and strokes
clopidogrel.warning: May increase bleeding risk. Inform doctor before surgery or dental procedures. Do not stop without doctor's advice.

telmisartan.name: Telmisartan
telmisartan.purpose: Used to treat high blood pressure and reduce cardiovascular risk
telmisartan.warning: May cause dizziness. Avoid potassium-rich foods in excess. Not safe during pregnancy.
